rulings.Case Title: MORGAN STANLEY SMITH BARNEY, LLC VS. ROELAND VAN KRIEKEN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition And Motion To Confirm Finra-Dr Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Thursday, August 15, 2024, Line 1. PETITIONER MORGAN STANLEY SMITH BARNEY, LLC's Hearing On Petition And Motion To Confirm Finra-Dr Arbitration Award. Granted as unopposed. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: LAUREN BRUNNER VS. TEDROS K. SIBHATU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Thursday, August 15, 2024, Line 2. DEFENDANT TEDROS SIBHATU AND SAN FRANCISCO INDEPENDENT TAXI ASSOCIATION's Motion To Dismiss. Defendants Tedros Sibhatu and San Francisco Independent Taxi Association's motion to dismiss is granted and denied in part. The motion is granted as to defendant San Francisco Independent Taxi Association. Plaintiff consents to dismissal of this entity, an insurance broker with no liability. (Opposition, 2:15-17.) The motion is denied as to defendant Sibhatu. On December 10, 2018, Sibhatu filed an answer and his thirteenth affirmative defense asserts "plaintiff's claims are barred and/or stayed due to federal bankruptcy laws." On December 27, 2018, Sibhatu doubled down on this assertion by filing a notice of stay of proceedings. In that notice, Sibhatu's counsel (Yuen) declared under penalty of perjury "Tedros K. Sibhatu is the party who requested or caused the stayThis case is stayed as follows: [w]ith regard to the following partiesDefendant Tedros K. Sibhatu." Sibhatu is bound by his judicial admissions and cannot now cynically argue that there was no stay. "The admission of fact in a pleading is a 'judicial admission. A judicial admission in a pleading is not merely evidence of a fact; it is a conclusive concession of the truth of the matterThe trial court may not ignore a judicial admission in a pleading, but must conclusively deem it true as against the pleader." (Bucur v. Ahmad (2016) 244 Cal.App.4th 175, 187.) In any event, even if Sibhatu's prior statements do not technically qualify as judicial admissions of "fact," the motion to dismiss is denied. The court concludes that the action was stayed and/or it was impracticable to bring this case to trial from December 27, 2018 (notice of bankruptcy stay) until July 1, 2024 (close of bankruptcy). (Rogers Decl., Ex. H; CCP 583.340(b), (c).) The mandatory 5-year period therefore has yet to run. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH SEIDLER ET AL VS. WELLS FARGO CLEARING SERVICES, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion By Respondent And Cross-Petitioner Wells Fargo Clearing Services Llc'S On Its Petition To Confirm Finra Arbitration A Ward Against Joseph Seidler; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; Declaration Of Tmothy N. Will
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Thursday, August 15, 2024, Line 11. DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO CLEARING SERVICES, LLC's Motion By Respondent And Cross-Petitioner Wells Fargo Clearing Services Llc'S On Its Petition To Confirm Finra Arbitration Award Against Joseph Seidler. Continued to August 29, 2024. Wells Fargo's reply brief filed on August 9, 2024, refers to Seidler's opposition to Wells Fargo's petition to confirm. That opposition brief (and the Thomas declaration) purportedly explain the basis to deny confirmation and grant vacation of the arbitration award under Armendariz v Foundation Health Psychcare Services Inc. (2000) 24 Cal.4th 83. Seidler attempted to file the opposition on August 6, 2024, but the clerk rejected it. The brief is therefore not on file with the court. The motion to vacate and motion to confirm are continued to August 29, 2024, to give Seidler an opportunity to file the opposition. The opposition shall be filed with the court by August 19, 2024. The parties are reminded of local rule 2.7B requiring courtesy copies of all papers. Please provide courtesy copies of all filings with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts.
